events.description:
In the upcoming WNBA game, scheduled for July 3 at 10:00PM ET:
If the Chicago Sky win, the market will resolve to "Chicago Sky".
If the Las Vegas Aces win, the market will resolve to "Las Vegas Aces".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: The matchup between the Chicago Sky and Las Vegas Aces features a balanced implied probability near 50 percent for the Sky, reflecting recent roster challenges and home-court dynamics offsetting the Aces' superior 11-4 record and stronger conference standing. The Sky sit at 4-10 amid multiple long-term absences, including Rickea Jackson's season-ending ACL tear, alongside uncertain timelines for DiJonai Carrington and Courtney Vandersloot, which have limited depth and contributed to a four-game losing streak. Las Vegas maintains momentum after clinching a Commissioner's Cup finals berth, led by A'ja Wilson and consistent scoring, though minor injury questions around players like Alysha Clark add slight uncertainty. Recent form, travel factors, and potential lineup adjustments from official injury reports could shift trader consensus ahead of the June 28 contest.

events.description:
In the upcoming WNBA game, scheduled for July 5 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Indiana Fever win, the market will resolve to "Indiana Fever".
If the Las Vegas Aces win, the market will resolve to "Las Vegas Aces".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Both squads enter with strong offensive talent and playoff aspirations, creating balanced trader pricing around 50% for the Fever. The Aces hold the league’s best record near 12-4, anchored by A’ja Wilson and home-court factors, yet they recently dropped a lopsided contest to the Wings that exposed defensive vulnerabilities. Indiana sits at 9-7 with Caitlin Clark listed probable amid ongoing back management, while key contributors like Aliyah Boston have dealt with lower-leg issues; the Fever’s recent form and perimeter shooting keep them competitive on the road. Additional roster health updates or rest decisions for either side’s stars could quickly shift implied probabilities before tip-off.

eventMetadata.context_description: The matchup between the Las Vegas Aces and Portland Fire sits at even implied odds largely because the Fire host the contest at Moda Center, where the expansion side has shown flashes of home resilience amid an 8-10 start. The Aces enter at 12-5 with proven depth from A'ja Wilson, Jackie Young, and Chelsea Gray, yet recent results include a road loss to the Liberty and an earlier defeat at Dallas that highlighted occasional execution lapses on the second night of back-to-backs. Portland has notched narrow victories over Seattle and Dallas while leaning on efficient guard play and frontcourt rebounding to stay competitive against stronger opponents. Any shift in availability for Wilson's interior dominance or a Portland hot-shooting night from beyond the arc could quickly tilt sentiment, as could fatigue factors if either club faces a compressed schedule window.

events.description:
The 2026 North Las Vegas mayoral election will be held on November 3, 2026, to elect the mayor of North Las Vegas, NV.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who becomes the next mayor of North Las Vegas as a result of this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by April 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting; however, if there is any ambiguity in the results, this market will resolve according to official information from the State of Nevada, Clark County, and the City of North Las Vegas.

events.title: 2026 North Las Vegas Mayoral Election Winner

events.description:
In the upcoming WNBA game, scheduled for July 11 at 6:00PM ET:
If the Phoenix Mercury win, the market will resolve to "Phoenix Mercury".
If the Las Vegas Aces win, the market will resolve to "Las Vegas Aces".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: The closely contested 50% implied probability for the Phoenix Mercury reflects a matchup shaped by contrasting records—Aces at 13-5 sit near the top of the Western Conference while the Mercury stand at 7-13—offset by injuries and recent form. Las Vegas has leaned on A’ja Wilson’s scoring outbursts and defensive pressure, as seen in their 86-76 win over Phoenix on June 17, yet multiple absences including Janiah Barker (leg), Chennedy Carter (illness), and Dana Evans (leg) thin their rotation. The Mercury counter with depth from Alyssa Thomas and improved perimeter shooting in spots, though hampered by outs such as Monique Akoa Makani (hamstring) and Natasha Mack (foot/knee). Home-court advantage for the July 11 contest at Michelob ULTRA Arena and both clubs’ turnover issues create the current balance, with any return-to-health news or back-to-back fatigue likely to shift trader consensus.

markets.description:

If the Las Vegas Aces win, the market will resolve to "Las Vegas Aces".

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

events.description:
In the upcoming WNBA game, scheduled for July 12 at 9:00PM ET:
If the Indiana Fever win, the market will resolve to "Indiana Fever".
If the Las Vegas Aces win, the market will resolve to "Las Vegas Aces".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Both teams enter the July 5 matchup with comparable records and star power driving the even 50% implied probability. The Fever’s high-powered offense, led by Caitlin Clark’s facilitation alongside scoring depth from Aliyah Boston and Kelsey Mitchell, has produced multiple 100-point games recently, while the Aces counter with A’ja Wilson’s interior dominance and a superior 13-5 mark. Home-court factors at T-Mobile Arena and Las Vegas’s defensive structure create balance against Indiana’s road challenges and Clark’s availability after earlier back concerns. Late roster or injury updates involving either star, along with shooting efficiency trends or rest advantages, remain the primary variables that could shift trader consensus in either direction.

events.description:
This market refers to the Major League Pickleball team matchup between Las Vegas Night Owls and Team Australia at Edward Jones Mid-Season Tournament, scheduled for July 8 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Las Vegas Night Owls' if Las Vegas Night Owls wins the overall team matchup against Team Australia.

This market will resolve to 'Team Australia' if Team Australia wins the overall team matchup against Las Vegas Night Owls.

If the matchup is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead. All markets will settle based on the official final result as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. Revisions to officially declared final scores made after market resolution will not be accounted for in determining the outcome.

events.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty Upper bracket quarterfinal 4 match between FaZe Vegas and G2 Minnesota in the Call of Duty League Championship Playoffs, initially scheduled for July 16 at 6:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "FaZe Vegas" if FaZe Vegas win the match against G2 Minnesota.

This market will resolve to "G2 Minnesota" if G2 Minnesota win the match against FaZe Vegas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: FaZe Vegas enters the Call of Duty League Championship Playoffs with strong recent momentum after claiming the 2026 Major II title and posting competitive results through Major IV, backed by a veteran roster featuring Simp, Drazah, 04, and Abuzah. As hosts of the July 16-19 double-elimination event at Las Vegas’s Michelob Ultra Arena, they benefit from home-crowd support and familiarity with the environment. G2 Minnesota has shown inconsistency across the season, with mid-pack finishes in recent majors and a roster led by Envoy, Skyz, and Kremp that has struggled to sustain high-level series wins. Historical head-to-head results remain mixed, though FaZe’s form and situational advantages shape current trader consensus around their edge in the best-of-five series.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "FaZe Vegas" if FaZe Vegas win Game 1 against G2 Minnesota.

This market will resolve to "G2 Minnesota" if G2 Minnesota win Game 1 against FaZe Vegas.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 1. 

If Game 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty Upper bracket quarterfinal 4 match between FaZe Vegas and G2 Minnesota in the Call of Duty League Championship Playoffs, initially scheduled for July 16 at 6:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if FaZe Vegas and G2 Minnesota play 4 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 4 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
